item.title: Amp Customizer
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Unlike most other boutique amplifier manufacturers, we have a Custom Shop that will design and build any amplifier, cabinet, or combo to your specs, tonal tastes, and design preferences. We do our best to offer amplifiers that cover the needs of most players, but we understand that sometimes an artist may want something different. Some of the services we offer in our Custom Shop include:

  • Custom circuits - Don't see YOUR amp on our site? Let's make it for you. We can hand-make any style of amp, from the smoothest cleans to the highest gain you can imagine
  • Custom speaker cabinets, combos and head sleeves
  • Finished wood enclosures
  • Custom footswitches and interfaces
  • Custom artwork on the front or back panel of the amplifier
  We have built many custom pieces for artists in every genre, playing every style of music, so feel free to contact us at info@newvintageamps.com with any and all Custom Shop inquiries. Let's work together and build the rig of your dreams.

[bd_separator]

For Custom Shop orders, a 50% down payment is required to begin designing your amplifier, cabinet, or combo. Unfortunately, we cannot offer lead times or delivery dates for custom orders, as they are built around our standard production schedule.

[bd_separator]

item.title: H&B 50
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The amp that started it all

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] The H&B 50 was the first amplifier we ever designed. Introduced in 2008, the H&B 50 is our best-selling guitar amp to date. Featuring a powerful clean channel and a snarling gain channel, this amp the definition of tonal versatility. We've always felt that many high-gain amps feature a clean channel that is more of an afterthought than a useable function, so we designed the H&B 50 to excel in both areas. Reminiscent of classic American designs, the clean channel showcases both a great top-end sparkle and a strong bottom-end. Stomp on the footswitch and you've got anything from a vintage British crunch to modern high-gain available in the second channel. The H&B 50 has great touch sensitivity, allowing you to clean up your tone or really bite down all with the way that you play your guitar. Add in the optional buffered effects loop and you've got a guitar amp that is a match for even the most demanding players. [/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"][/bd_col_6] [bd_separator]

technical specifications

 
  • 2 Channel Foot-Switchable High Gain Amplifier
  • 50 Watts of All Tube Power
  • Available As A Head Enclosure Or 2x12 Combo
  • Channel 1 is Clean while Channel 2 is Modern High Gain
  • Tube Configuration: 5AR4, 2-EL34 or 2-6L6, 4-12AX7
  • Model Can Be Easily Biased to Use EL34's or 6L6's
  • Switchable tube or solid state rectifier for classic or modern tones
  • Impedance Switch for 4, 8, and 16 Ohm Cabinets
  • Controls Include: Channel 1 (clean): Volume, Treble, Mid, Bass, & Brightness
  • Channel 2 (gain): Gain, Volume, Treble, Mid, & Bass - (shares Brightness with Ch. 1)
  • Hand Wired, using Point to Point and eyelet board construction techniques

item.title: SA-45
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Rooted in heart of classic american tone

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] an amp that nails the classic American country, blues, and rock tones can sing or howl round, warm tone that you can bite when needed drive switch introduces more of a midrange bite and growl, changing from clean to overdriven tones depending on the player's touch amp really sings when played by a dynamic guitarist a rare example of an amp that is influenced by the player its balanced tone provides the ideal foundation for pedals [/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"][/bd_col_6] [bd_separator]

Technical Specifications

 
  • Single Channel American voiced Amplifier
  • Available as a 1-12, 2-10, or 2-12 Combo or Head Enclosure
  • 45 Watts of Power
  • Tube Configuration: 5AR4, 2-EL34 or 2-6L6, 3-12AX7
  • Controls Include: Volume, Gain, Drive, Treble, Mid, Bass, Brightness
  • Model Can Be Easily Biased to Use EL34's or 6L6's
  • Impedance Switch for 4, 8, and 16 Ohm Cabinets
  • Drive Switch On Front Panel Changes Preamp Midrange Voicing and Gain

item.title: Cables
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The missing link to your perfect tone

[bd_separator]

We make the highest quality instrument and audio cables you will find, made with the most reliable and tonally pure wire and ends. All of our cables are road-worthy and studio-quality. We don't make any compromises with your tone, so why should you?

[bd_separator]

[bd_col_4 class="alpha"]

Instrument Cable

Our instrument cables are dead silent. No added interference, static, or noise from moving around on stage. Properly terminated Canare or Mogami cables with rock-solid Neutrik plugs make our cables nearly indestructible. These are the only cables we use in the shop because they allow us to hear our amps with NO added noise.

[/bd_col_4] [bd_col_4]

Patch Cable

Our heavy duty patch cables promote clarity throughout your pedalboard. Made from Mogami or Canare cable with premium Amphenol or Neutrik connectors, these cables will hold up while on the road and allow you to focus on what matters most - your performance.

[/bd_col_4] [bd_col_4 class="omega"]

Speaker Cable

The connection between your tube amplifier and speaker is the most important connection in your rig. Not all speaker cables are created equal. Our speaker cables are made out of rugged Canare cable and jumbo connectors to ensure durability that stands the test of time. We use a special kind of cable that acts much like a humbucker and reduces unwanted noise that many speaker cables add between your amplifier and speaker. Think of this as your last line of defense against noise.

[/bd_col_4] [bd_col_6 class="alpha"]

Body Pack Cable

Most body pack cables can't stand up to the abuse they see on the road. Ours are designed to withstand the beating you give them each night and out-perform those you'll find at other music and tour gear stores.

[/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"]

XLR Cable

Need a bullet-proof mic cable for your stage or studio? Look no further than these. Utilizing the fantastic line of Neutrik connectors and Canare's line of star-quad cable, you'll have a great sounding interconnect with superb noise performance up 10 times better than a regular microphone cable.

[/bd_col_6] [bd_separator]

If you have any questions regarding our cables and accessories, or would like to place an order, please email us at info@newvintageamps.com

Our line of Undertow bass amplifiers is redefining the way bass guitar is heard. The Undertow series delivers dynamic, punchy tone with enough low-end response to level the venue you're playing.

item.title: FAQ
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Your company name doesn't make sense, new/old amps?

Actually, it makes perfect sense; the name comes from the fact that we're building amplifiers like back in the good 'ol days--wiring everything by hand on eyelet boards and using the best components we can find. There are less and less amps on the market made this way. With these "vintage" or old school build techniques, we're creating "new" and modern tones. At the same time, our gear still has a vintage flavor that comes from building things the old way. With that said, we don't clone or try to emulate any other amplifier--there's plenty of guys doing that already!

[bd_separator]

Why should I buy a New Vintage Amplifier over a “(name brand here)”?

Sure, we're totally biased with this one. One of the biggest things you get with a New Vintage over many other brands is a quality that is difficult to match. We like to refer to them as “heirloom amps” because the build is tailored to outlast even the vintage classics. The number one thing we avoid is cutting corners-each amplifier is hand wired, using the finest grade components available. We have personalized customer service that is also difficult to find in the larger manufacturers. All of us who work for New Vintage are players, and with that we have a desire to provide our customers with the greatest tone possible.

[bd_separator]

How long will it take to get my amplifier after I place my order?

Currently, New Vintage Amplifiers are made to order. Typical production lead time for an amplifier is about 14-16 weeks. If you order a custom piece - anything other than a standard product found on the website - your piece will be subject to different terms that we will outline on a case-by-case basis.

[bd_separator]

Why don't New Vintage Amplifiers have (name modern amp gimmick here)?

We have a philosophy here at New Vintage HQ, which is “Simpler is ALWAYS better.” Any electronics textbook will tell you that the less your signal has to pass through, the better. We keep our circuits as simple as possible, all the while providing you with killer tone.

[bd_separator]

Can I get custom colors for my tolex and grill cloth?

Yep! We have all different colors of tolex, grill cloth and piping. Just ask us about our current selections, or check out our Customizer page! [bd_separator]

Can I get a different speaker loaded in my cabinet?

Yes you can! We feel pretty strongly about the tone and quality of Celestion Speakers and how they match up with our amps, but if you have a favorite speaker that you just can't live without, let us know. We can get Jensen, Celestion, Sica, Weber, Mojo, Eminence, and Electro-Harmonix. Note that a custom speaker order will probably change the price of your amp.

[bd_separator]

item.title: Contact
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Shop Hours:

All shop time is by-appointment only.  

If you have any questions regarding the various amps, cabinets, and accessories we offer, or want to discuss an artist endorsement opportunity, please email us at info@newvintageamps.com

[bd_separator]

item.title: Retribution 30
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Where classic American and British tones meet

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] The Retribution 30 is our combination of tweed and British tones. Naturally a little darker, the Retribution 30 is designed to produce a tone that is British-inspired but without the squawky, brittle tone. The Retribution 30 features a unique Drive switch that, when off, has a round, warm clean to classic EL84 crunch. Flip the Drive switch on and it moves the midrange point for a more aggressive tone as well as increased gain and touch sensitivity. This amplifier is known for its unique and desirable distortion characteristic. The Retribution 30 is perfect for a player that is looking for a straight-forward, no frills amp. [/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"][/bd_col_6] [bd_separator]

Tone Samples

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] [bd_center]
[/bd_center] [/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"] [bd_center]
[/bd_center] [/bd_col_6] [bd_separator]

Retribution 30 Pricing

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"]

Headshell - 1x12 Combo - [/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"]

2x10 Combo - 2x12 Combo - [/bd_col_6] [bd_separator]

Technical Specifications

 
  • Single Channel British-Style Tube Amplifier
  • 30 Watts of Power
  • Tube Configuration: 5U4, 4-EL84, 3-12AX7
  • Controls Include: Volume, Gain, Drive, Treble, Mid, Bass, Cut
  • Drive Switch On Front Panel Changes Preamp Midrange Voicing and Gain
  • Impedance Switch for 4, 8, and 16 Ohm Cabinets With 2 Parallel Outs

item.title: VL-Series
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Simpler is better

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] The V-15L and V-7L offer a wide range of tonal options but is incredibly light on features. With just three controls on the V-15L (Volume, Tone, and Master) and two on the V-7L (Volume and Tone) - these amps are perfect for that simple vintage feel. A parallel triode input stage allows the V-Series to go from fat cleans to rich overdrive with ease. The Tone control is a tilt-style design - turn it to the right and you dial up more treble or turn it to the left for more low-end. This Tone control can move the amps from warm and round to bright and crunchy tones. Designed to be a simple and short path from guitar to speaker, these amps are incredibly pedal-friendly. And don't worry about volume; with custom-designed Mercury Magnetics transformers, the V-15L is loud enough to stand out at any gig. The V-7L is great for studio, bedroom, or small venue use. [/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"] [/bd_col_6] [bd_separator] [bd_col_6 class="alpha"]

item.title: MK Series
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The AC-inspired amp killer

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] The MK36 was created for a musician that was looking for the perfect complement to his Retribution 30 - something that was a bit brighter with the ability to dial up a multitude of different tones. We decided to do something that other boutique manufacturers don't - expand upon the AC-style amp platform, not copy from it. The first thing we did was internally jump the Normal and Bright channels and allow the player to mix the two to their liking. The Normal and Bright controls not only blend the qualities of the two channels, but together they fatten up the tone and provide a wider soundstage that the two channels individually cannot achieve. The channels can be used individually, but they're better blended together. In a traditional AC circuit, the Normal channel is almost useless as it doesn't go through any sort of tone stack. We spiced up the Normal channel on the MK series by routing it through the tone controls and adding a much-needed Midrange control. We also made the tone bolder and tighter than a traditional AC-based amplifier. The transformers are the true secret to this amplifier. Based on the prized amps of two of the most influential players in American music history, these transformers push the MK36 to head of the AC-inspired pack. [/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"] [/bd_col_6] [bd_separator]

Tone Samples

  [bd_separator]

MK36 Pricing

 

Headshell - $2,450 1x12 Combo - $2,825 2x12 Combo - $3,050 Buffered effects loop - +$95

[bd_center] Please Email us at info@newvintageamps.com for pricing and availability. [/bd_center] [bd_separator]

Technical Specifications

 
  • Single Channel British-Style Tube Amplifier
  • 36 Watts of Power
  • Tube Configuration: 5AR4, 4-EL84, 3-12AX7
  • Controls Include: Volume, Bright gain, Normal gain, Treble, Midrange, Bass and Cut
  • The Bright and Normal "channels" are connected internally and can be blended together or used separately, allowing for 3 different voices from the preamp
  • Both channels when used independently have full access to EQ controls
  • Impedance Switch for 4, 8, and 16 Ohm Cabinets With 2 Parallel Outs
  • Dimensions (1x12 combo): 25W x 10D x 20-1/4H
[bd_separator] [bd_center] Interested in purchasing this piece? Email us at info@newvintageamps.com to place your order. [/bd_center]

item.title: CJ-18
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
[bd_col_6 class="alpha"]

It isn't often that we have a piece finished, for sale, and ready to ship. This was a custom amp made for an artist, and during the build process, he proposed to his girlfriend and decided to finance a wedding instead of buy new gear. We couldn't be happier for him! We hate seeing this sit in the shop all day, so we are letting it go for a steal! This is the only CJ-18 in existence, and we don't plan on making it a production model. The amp is an 18 Watt 2x10 combo featuring single and parallel inputs, fixed or cathode bias switch, negative feedback switch, and Celestion's 10 inch versions of the Greenback and Vintage 30. Volume, Gain, Treble, Mid, Bass, and Cut controls give you full tone-shaping capabilities.

The CJ-18 would normally sell for $1,900. Make us an offer at info@newvintageamps.com.

[/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"] [/bd_col_6]

item.title: Custom Cab Contest
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Who likes free stuff?

View Mobile-Friendly Contest Page

This giveaway is pretty simple - design YOUR custom cabinet in the Customizer below, take a screen shot of the cabinet, and share it on your social media pages (Twitter, Instagram, Facebook) with the hashtag #CustomCabContest and tag us in the post. That's it. There are no limits on the number of entries you can submit as long as you abide by the previously mentioned rules. One lucky person will win the cabinet that they designed and we'll give it to them. How rad is that? Very.

You can find us on Facebook, Twitter, and Instagram by searching "New Vintage Amps".

[bd_separator] [bd_col_6 class="alpha"]

RULES

  1. Design your cabinet using the Customizer and options below.
  2. Take a screen shot of the cabinet
  3. Share it on your social media pages (Twitter, Instagram, Facebook) with the hashtag #CustomCabContest and tag us in the post
  4. Repeat
[/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"]

FINE PRINT

The winner will be randomly selected on January 31, 2014. We will notify the winner via the social media outlet upon which their winning submission was posted, so PLEASE follow us on Twitter and Instagram (@newvintageamps) and "like" us on Facebook. We will cover shipping to the lower 48 States. All other shipping will be sorted out between us and the winner. The winner will be contacted about speaker selection for the cabinet. [/bd_col_6] [bd_separator]

item.title: Post #2054
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
BLENDING AMERICAN & BRITISH TONES

The MK36 was created for a musician that was looking for the perfect complement to his Retribution 30 - something that was a bit brighter with the ability to dial up a multitude of different tones. We decided to do something that other boutique manufacturers don't - expand upon the AC-style amp platform, not copy from it. The first thing we did was internally jump the Normal and Bright channels and allow the player to mix the two to their liking. The Normal and Bright controls not only blend the qualities of the two channels, but together they fatten up the tone and provide a wider soundstage that the two channels individually cannot achieve. The channels can be used individually, but they're better blended together. In a traditional AC circuit, the Normal channel is almost useless as it doesn't go through any sort of tone stack. We spiced up the Normal channel on the MK series by routing it through the tone controls and adding a much-needed Midrange control. We also made the tone bolder and tighter than a traditional AC-based amplifier. The transformers are the true secret to this amplifier. Based on the prized amps of two of the most influential players in American music history, these transformers push the MK36 to head of the AC-inspired pack.

CK30 (shown with optional 2X12 cabinet) [bd_separator]

TONE SAMPLES

[bd_separator]

MK36 Pricing

Headshell - $2050
1x12 Combo - $2,350
2x12 Combo - $2,525
Buffered effects loop - +$95

[bd_separator]

Technical Specifications

 

  • Single Channel British-Style Tube Amplifier
  • 36 Watts of Power
  • Tube Configuration: 5AR4, 4-EL84, 3-12AX7
  • Controls Include: Volume, Bright gain, Normal gain, Treble, Midrange, Bass and Cut
  • The Bright and Normal "channels" are connected internally and can be blended together or used separately, allowing for 3 different voices from the preamp
  • Both channels when used independently have full access to EQ controls
  • Impedance Switch for 4, 8, and 16 Ohm Cabinets With 2 Parallel Outs
  • Dimensions (1x12 combo): 25W x 10D x 20-1/4H

MK36 was created for a musician that was looking for the perfect complement to his Retribution 30 - something that was a bit brighter with the ability to dial up a multitude of different tones. We decided to do something that other boutique manufacturers don't - expand upon the AC-style amp platform, not copy from it. The first thing we did was internally jump the Normal and Bright channels and allow the player to mix the two to their liking. The Normal and Bright controls not only blend the qualities of the two channels, but together they fatten up the tone and provide a wider soundstage that the two channels individually cannot achieve. The channels can be used individually, but they're better blended together. In a traditional AC circuit, the Normal channel is almost useless as it doesn't go through any sort of tone stack. We spiced up the Normal channel on the MK series by routing it through the tone controls and adding a much-needed Midrange control. We also made the tone bolder and tighter than a traditional AC-based amplifier. The transformers are the true secret to this amplifier. Based on the prized amps of two of the most influential players in American music history, these transformers push the MK36 to head of the AC-inspired MK36 was created for a musician that was looking for the perfect complement to his Retribution 30 - something that was a bit brighter with the ability to dial up a multitude of different tones. We decided to do something that other boutique manufacturers don't - expand upon the AC-style amp platform, not copy from it. The first thing we did was internally jump the Normal and Bright channels and allow the player to mix the two to their liking. The Normal and Bright controls not only blend the qualities of the two channels, but together they fatten up the tone and provide a wider soundstage that the two channels individually cannot achieve. The channels can be used individually, but they're better blended together. In a traditional AC circuit, the Normal channel is almost useless as it doesn't go through any sort of tone stack. We spiced up the Normal channel on the MK series by routing it through the tone controls and adding a much-needed Midrange control. We also made the tone bolder and tighter than a traditional AC-based amplifier. The transformers are the true secret to this amplifier. Based on the prized amps of two of the most influential players in American music history, these transformers push the MK36 to head of the AC-inspired separately, allowing for 3 different voices from the preamp</li> <li>Both channels when used independently have full access to EQ controls</li> <li>Impedance Switch for 4, 8, and 16 Ohm Cabinets With item.title: Undertow 300
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
You've never heard a bass amp like this before

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] The options for quality tube bass amplifiers are getting more limited each and every day. Most modern production bass amps lack the deep, responsive tone that every bass player - including ourselves - searches for. We couldn't find that tone anywhere we looked, so we built it ourselves. When we spent three years designing the Undertow 300 we sought to create the ultimate tube bass amp. We weeded out the endless options and gimmicks that flood the bass amp market and included just the right set of features to help shape the big, harmonically rich tone that our Undertow 300 is known for. Our all-tube circuitry, coupled with custom-designed transformers from Mercury Magnetics give the Undertow 300 the punch, dynamics, and warmth that other modern amps are missing. With an optional tube-driven balanced DI output, designed in conjunction with Bill Whitlock, President of Jensen Transformers and considered one of the pioneers of balanced audio lines, the Undertow 300 can easily fit any player's needs - from small clubs to massive arenas. Whether you prefer high-headroom cleans or an amp that growls and grinds, the Undertow 300 has your tone covered. [/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"] In the end we built an amp that WE wanted to play - hand-wired, point to point, and using the finest components available. The tone is unmatched and unmistakable, and the reason it has already graced stages around the world, used by artists from all genres and styles. The Undertow 300 is the tube amp that every bass player has been searching for. [/bd_col_6] [bd_separator]

Undertow 300 Pricing

 

Headshell - $3,795 Tube-buffered DI - +$195 Buffered effects loop - +$95

[bd_center] Please Email us at info@newvintageamps.com for pricing and availability. [/bd_center] [bd_separator]

Technical Specifications

 
  • Single channel all-tube bass guitar amplifier
  • Over 300W of power
  • Available as a head only
  • Custom designed Mercury Magnetics transformers
  • External bias test points and controls for rapid power tube changing
  • Tube configuration: 4-KT88, 1-6V6, 2-12AX7, 1-12AU7
  • Power tubes transformer driven with a 6V6 power tube for maximum output and punch
  • Fan cooled power amp for maximum tube life
  • Impedance switch selecting 2, 4 or 8 ohm output taps, with two parallel outputs, and one 1/4" unbalanced line-out (main outs are one combination Speakon/1/4" and one 1/4" TS jack)
  • Controls include: 0dB & -15dB inputs (for active/high output pickup bass guitars), Gain, Bright Switch, Treble, Midrange, Bass, Active Mid, 5-Way Mid Frequency Switch, Bass, and Master Volume
  • Jensen transformer-balanced XLR direct-out and buffered effects loop available upon request
  • Completely hand-wired using point to point and eyelet board construction techniques
  • Dimensions (L,W,H): 25" x 12" x 10"
[bd_separator] [bd_center] Interested in purchasing this piece? Email us at info@newvintageamps.com to place your order. [/bd_center]

item.title: Undertow 160
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The perfect bass amp for any venue

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] When we set out to design the Undertow 160 we began with a radical idea - how can we generate the most power out of as few power tubes as possible? This question took us three years to figure out, and the results are eye-opening. We came up with a unique design that borrows from long-forgotten hi-fi techniques, allowing the Undertow 160 to draw an incredible amount of power, weight, and tone out of two power tubes. Hand- wired all-tube circuitry and custom-designed Mercury Magnetics transformers provide an immediate and full bass tone that we feel is missing from most modern production bass amplifiers. The Undertow 160 is ideal for the player looking to manage stage volume while delivering a defined, harmonically-rich, and articulate bass tone. When paired with the optional tube-driven balanced DI output, the Undertow 160 can handle any stage or performance situation it faces. The Undertow 160 can suit any artist and any style of music with its dynamic preamp that can range from smooth cleans to gritty overdrive. The Undertow 160 has every player covered. [/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"] [/bd_col_6] [bd_separator]

Undertow 160 Pricing

 

Headshell - $3,295 Tube-buffered DI - +$195 Buffered effects loop - +$95

[bd_center] [/bd_center] [bd_separator]

Technical Specifications

 
  • Single channel all-tube bass guitar amplifier
  • Over 160W of power
  • Available as a head only
  • Custom designed Mercury Magnetics transformers
  • External bias test points and controls for rapid power tube changing
  • Tube configuration: 2-KT88, 1-6V6, 2-12AX7, 1-12AU7
  • Power tubes transformer driven with a 6V6 power tube for maximum output and punch
  • Fan cooled power amp for optimal tube operating conditions
  • Impedance switch selecting 2, 4 or 8 ohm output taps, with two parallel outputs, and one 1/4" unbalanced line-out (main outs are one combination Speakon/1/4" and one 1/4" TS jack)
  • Controls include: 0dB & -15dB inputs (for active/high output pickup bass guitars), Gain, Bright Switch, Treble, Midrange, Bass, Active Mid, 5-Way Mid Frequency Switch, Bass, and Master Volume
  • Jensen transformer-balanced XLR direct-out and buffered effects loop available upon request
  • Completely hand-wired using point to point and eyelet board construction techniques
  • Dimensions (L,W,H): 25" x 12" x 10"
  • Weight: 57 pounds
[bd_separator] [bd_center] Interested in purchasing this model? Email us at info@newvintageamps.com to place your order. [/bd_center]

item.title: Deception 60
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The ultimate in tonal options

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] Modern high gain is something of a rarity in the hand-wired amplifier market. Few manufacturers actually hand-wire anything with this many options or this much gain. Multi-channel high gain amplifiers take a rare amount of discipline and patience to hand-build with the precision and noise performance New Vintage guitar amps are known for. The Deception 60 is our 2.5 channel take on an amp that is the ultimate in tonal options. The Deception 60 offers a fully-featured American-voiced clean channel that is dynamic and full with the right amount of top-end sparkle to cut through the mix. You'll never find a clean channel this rich and punchy on a high gain amplifier. Channel 2 is ideal for an overdriven rhythm tone with a thick British crunch that is tight and impactful when palm muted. Channel 3 is the modern high gain show-stopper that is smooth, articulate, and percussive. The completely saturated tone has incredible note definition, sustain and clarity. Channels 2 and 3 share an EQ, but have independent Volume and Gain controls. Global Resonance and Bright controls shape the overall feel and weight, allowing you to dial in a tone perfect for your style of play. No matter what sound you're going for, the Deception 60 has your bases covered. [/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"] [/bd_col_6] [bd_separator]

item.title: Guitar Cabinets
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
A guitar speaker cabinet for every player

 

[bd_center] Our attention to detail in both design and construction sets New Vintage guitar speaker cabinets apart from the rest. Built in-house, our 1x12, 2x12, and 4x12 guitar cabinets are constructed with 15mm BB/BB grade, void-free Baltic Birch and all-metal hardware to ensure long-lasting durability and the best tone. All of our cabinets are designed to work with the speakers that we load them with. Thoughtful design, industry-leading materials, and careful craftsmanship give our cabinets a larger, tighter sound than others available on the market. Our 2x12 and 4x12 cabinets come standard with a Plug and Play switching system that allows you to select between 16 and 4 Ohm mono inputs or isolate the left and right set of speakers for stereo 8 Ohm inputs. Our cabinets come standard with your choice of Celestion's Classic Series line of speakers, but we can load it with any speaker from Celestion's AlNiCo or Heritage lines, as well as Weber, Eminence, WGS, Mojo, or Jensen speakers. Prices may vary for speakers outside of Celestion's Classic Series. [/bd_center]

item.title: Bass Cabinets
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Think all bass cabinets are created equal?

 

[bd_center] We don't build bass speaker cabinets like anyone else. We design, hand-build, and test all of our cabinets in-house one at a time to ensure the quality of every piece we make. Unlike other manufacturers, we design our cabinets around the speakers that we use and not the other way around. All of our bass cabs are ported Thiele/Small designs that are centered around Celestion's great line of bass drivers, giving our cabs extended bass frequency response and maximum potential from the speakers that you don't get with sealed or infinite baffle cabinets. All-metal hardware and 15mm imported BB/BB grade, void-free Baltic Birch make our cabs road-worthy and nearly indestructible. All of our bass cabinets include two Neutrik SpeakON-1/4 inch combo jacks for the most solid connection possible. Still think all bass cabinets are created equal? [/bd_center]

item.title: H&B Series
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The amp that started it all

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] The H&B 50 was the first amplifier we ever designed. Introduced in 2008, the H&B 50 is our best-selling guitar amp to date. Featuring a powerful clean channel and a snarling gain channel, this amp is the definition of tonal versatility. We've always felt that many high-gain amps feature a clean channel that is more of an afterthought than a useable function, so we designed the H&B 50 to excel in both areas. Reminiscent of classic American designs, the clean channel showcases both a great top-end sparkle and a strong bottom-end. Stomp on the footswitch and you've got anything from a vintage British crunch to modern high-gain available in the second channel. The H&B 50 has great touch sensitivity, allowing you to clean up your tone or really bite down all with the way that you play your guitar. Add in the optional buffered effects loop and you've got a guitar amp that is a match for even the most demanding players. Also available in a 25 Watt model. [/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"][/bd_col_6] [bd_separator]

item.title: SA Series
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Rooted in heart of classic american tone

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] The SA-45 and lower-wattage SA-25 are two amps that nail the classic American Country, Blues, and Rock tones. The SA Series of amplifiers have a nice round, warm tone that can bite when needed. The drive switch introduces more midrange growl, changing from clean to overdriven tones depending largely on the player's touch. These amps are designed to be able to sing or howl depending on how you play. This is a rare example of an amp that is influenced by the player. The SA series of amps really shine when played by a dynamic guitarist. The balanced tone provides the ideal foundation for pedals and effects. [/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"] [/bd_col_6] [bd_separator]

item.title: Rogue 50
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Stock or hot-rodded? You choose.

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] Most amplifiers that are used to achieve that iconic British lead tone are one-trick ponies. We knew that we could produce an amp that was capable of more. The Rogue 50 is a multi-dimensional single-channel amp that offers both stock and modded British tones that are able to be switched at a moment's notice. We began with the concept of a jumped dual-channel amp and expanded it. The Rogue 50 is a single input amplifier that passively mixes two isolated channels (Normal and Bright) so that they can be blended to your taste. This passive mixing gives you access to tones that you couldn't otherwise get out of a channel-jumped amp. To add to the versatility we added a Cascade mode - when engaged, the amp takes the otherwise parallel gain stages of the Normal and Bright channels and puts them in series, one in front of the other, to increase gain saturation, add richer harmonics, and deliver that classic hot-rodded tone. The Cascade mode can go from a harmonically-rich crunch to saturated, smooth leads. This amp is a dream in the studio. You can use the Normal and Bright channels on their own, together, or in Cascade mode. The Rogue 50 can also put out a bold clean tone that features a throaty midrange with lots of top-end sheen and a thick bottom end that makes any 4x12 thump. Why settle on one British-voiced amp when you can have them all in one box? [/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"] [/bd_col_6] [bd_separator]

Rogue 50 Pricing

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"]

Headshell - $2,350 1x12 Combo - $2,750

[/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"]

2x12 Combo - $2,950 Buffered effects loop - +$95

[/bd_col_6] [bd_center] Please Email us at info@newvintageamps.com for pricing and availability. [/bd_center] [bd_separator]

Technical Specifications

 
  • Single Channel Mid-Gain Amplifier
  • 50 Watts of Power
  • Tube Configuration: 2-EL34, 3-12AX7
  • Controls Include: Volume (pull for Cascade), Bright Gain, Normal Gain, Treble, Mid, Bass, Body, Brilliance
  • Very Touch Sensitive: Go anywhere from silky cleans, to crunchy high gain tones with just the turn of your guitar's volume knob.
  • Impedance Switch for 4, 8, and 16 Ohm Cabinets With 2 Parallel Outs
[bd_separator] [bd_center] Interested in purchasing this piece? Email us at info@newvintageamps.com to place your order. [/bd_center]

item.title: ÆGEAN SERIES
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Classic reverb tone: reinvented

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] The ÆGEAN 40 and ÆGEAN 20 are our first amplifiers to feature onboard reverb. We have received requests for years to build an amp with reverb, but we always had our reservations. Reverb circuits are inherently noisy, tone sucking, and impact the lows and highs in an amp. Since its introduction into guitar amplification, no one thought to address these issues, but we did. We began by designing the amp around the reverb tank, believing that reverb in an amp is just as crucial to the tone as any other component of the amplifier. We changed the way the reverb tank connects to the amplifier, in turn greatly improving noise performance and lowering idle noise. We changed the way that the reverb is switched on and off with a new method that doesn't run your audio signal 20 feet outside of your amp and back. By implementing a handful of major design changes, we've opened up a world of increased performance and depth previously unheard of in a reverb circuit. The ÆGEAN series of amplifiers offer reverb that can be set anywhere from a subtle reflection to full-on cavernous, effect-like waves. With the Reverb (Mix) and Dwell (Intensity) controls, you can carve out an incredible array of reverb effects unlike any other amp available. Reverb aside, these amps possess the strongest, most powerful clean tone we've ever created. Start opening up the Gain control and the amp begins to break up nicely. The ÆGEAN 40 and ÆGEAN 20 have the most useable reverb and boldest tone of any amplifier available today. [/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"] [/bd_col_6] [bd_separator]

Tone Samples

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] [/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"] [/bd_col_6] [bd_separator] [bd_col_6 class="alpha"]

item.title: Undertow 500D
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
All of the tone - none of the weight

 

A design philosophy we follow at New Vintage Amplifiers is, "performance over price." Time after time, we have seen our competitors utilize new solid state technologies to put out cheap but powerful bass amps that are light weight. We saw the benefits that these amps were providing to bass players everywhere, but we felt that nothing had yet opened up the full potential of this technology. We saw this as an opportunity to use state-of-the-art components to take a bass amplifier a few steps further. By combining our passion for point-to-point, hand-wired tube circuits with the latest solid state technology, we have managed to create an amplifier that has every ounce of tone you'd find in our all-tube bass lineup, but with almost none of the weight - the Undertow 500D.

We wanted to use the most transparent yet organic-sounding power amp possible to ensure that the warmth and dynamics of the all-tube preamp came through your speakers uninhibited. We are the first to admit that we were shocked to find that the Undertow 500D had all of the deep, clear bass and growling, gritty breakup that is present in our all-tube Undertow series, but with the benefits of a solid state power section; the amp is light-weight, has the immediacy you'd expect from a high-end solid state amplifier, and great tone at any volume. Adding our optional tube-driven Jensen Transformer balanced direct out, you have a versatile package that can even be used as a stand-alone tube bass preamp! No speaker cabinet needed!

This is our best example of the merging of vintage build quality with the newest technology available. We have created a true hybrid of vintage hand-wired, vacuum tube amplification and modern, cutting edge solid state technologies. The Undertow 500D is truly unique in the bass amplifier market, and will make you rethink what solid state technology can sound like.

[bd_separator] [bd_col_4 class="alpha"] [/bd_col_4] [bd_col_4] [/bd_col_4] [bd_col_4 class="omega"] [/bd_col_4] [bd_separator]

Tone Samples

[bd_separator]

Undertow 500D Pricing

 

2U rack amplifier - Email for availability Tube-buffered DI - Buffered effects loop - Custom tolexed 2U head sleeve -

[bd_separator]

Technical Specifications

 
  • Single channel true tube hybrid bass guitar amplifier
  • Over 500 Watts of power in an astoundingly light 13 pound package
  • Preamplifier and Linear Power Supply are completely hand-wired point-to-point on an eyelet board
  • Available as a 2U rack enclosure with optional tolexed head sleeve
  • Laser-engraved brushed black anodized front panel
  • Custom designed Mercury Magnetics transformer
  • Tube configuration: 2-12AX7, 1-12AU7
  • State-of-the-art Class D power amp
  • 550W @ 1% THD+N, 1kHz, 4Ω
  • Features an advanced soft clip circuit with front panel indication to prevent harsh power amp clipping
  • Featuring built-in under-volage, thermal, high frequency, and over-current (shorted speaker) power amp protections
  • Frequency Response: 20Hz - 20kHz, all loads, +/- 0.5dB
  • Fan cooled power amp for maximum performance and output
  • Dual Neutrik SpeakOn output jacks
  • Self-adjusting output stable down to 2 Ohms - Unheard of for Class D bass amplifiers
  • Controls include: 0dB/-15dB switchable input pad, Gain, Bright Switch, Treble, Midrange, Bass, Active Mid, 5-Way Mid Frequency Switch, Bass, and Master Volume
  • Optional tube-driven Jensen transformer-balanced XLR direct-out allows for use as a stand-alone bass preamp
  • Optional buffered effects loop
  • Optional mute switch
  • Dimensions (W,H,D): 19" x 3.5" x 12"
[bd_separator] [bd_center] Interested in purchasing this amplifier? Email us at info@newvintageamps.com to place your order. [/bd_center]

item.title: How To Buy
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Ordering Your New Vintage Amplifier

Due to the vast cosmetic options that we offer for our products, you can purchase your new amplifier, cabinet, or combo direct from us. We occasionally have prebuilt items at select dealers, which will be listed here on our website. Stocking dealers can also accept custom orders. We build each piece to order, so you have the ability to select your tolex, grill cloth, and hardware colors - to truly make this amplifier yours. We require a 50% down payment to begin your build, with the remainder of the balance due upon completion of the products, prior to shipping. The down payment is used to purchase cut-to-length cosmetic materials (tolex and grill cloth), custom transformers, speakers, engineering/design costs (if the build being discussed is non-standard) and admin costs for the build. **Due to the custom nature of our products, your down payment is an agreement to complete the purchase and is non-refundable.** If you have any questions regarding our ordering process, please don't hesitate to contact us.

Building the best amplifiers and cabinets we can takes time, so please be patient throughout the build cycle. Believe it when we say that no one wants you to have your New Vintage amplifier or cabinet more than we do! Please contact us for our current production lead time. We try to be as flexible possible though to try to work around touring schedules, gigs, recording, etc...

[bd_separator]

Paying for Your New Vintage Amplifier

We offer a wide variety of payment options to make the purchasing process easier for you. We gladly accept cash, Visa, MasterCard, Discover, cashier's check, bank transfer, wire transfer (fees may apply), Square Cash, PayPal, and Venmo. If paying with a personal check, please be aware that we will not ship any product until the funds have cleared. We do also charge a $50 fee in the event that a check does not clear due to insufficient funds. Unfortunately, we're not able to accept trade-ins towards your purchase at this time.

[bd_separator]

Payment Plans

If you would like to make incremental payments towards one of our products, we will allow you to do so, interest-free, for a period of 12 months. Once you have paid in full, we will insert you into the build queue and begin your piece as soon as we can. We will not ship an order until we have received payments totaling the full purchase price. All orders are then subject to our current lead times. If, for any reason, the total balance is not paid in full within the 12-month window provided from the date of down payment, we will retain 50% of the payments received (beyond the non-refundable down payment) to offset the costs of your build. In short, to get your product you have to pay for it.

[bd_separator]

Shipping

We prefer to ship via FedEx, but will ship with UPS upon request. All speaker cabinets larger than our NV 2x12 guitar cabinets ship via truck freight. Shipping costs are calculated at the completion of the product(s), but we're happy to give you a rough estimate if needed.

[bd_separator]

International Orders

We currently do not have any international distribution or dealers outside of the USA. We handle international orders and shipping on a case-by-case basis as rates, taxes, and possible fees change with each order. Feel free to contact us with any questions regarding international orders and shipping. Please note that large items (such as bass cabinets) can be VERY costly to ship overseas.

item.title: Become a New Vintage Artist
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
When you become a New Vintage Amplifiers artist, you get a lot more than just a great amp. One of the things that we pride ourselves on, and one of the reasons we have such a great artist roster, is our customer service. We understand that every customer is just as valuable as the last, and we strive to provide excellence in everything we do. From building the most reliable, tour-worthy amplifiers, answering emails, or helping out when problems arise on the road, we know the value of great service. All too often we hear of musicians that can't get help from other gear companies, pieces that go down on every tour, and a lack of transparency between the builder and the artist. We have an incredible relationship with all of our artists that goes beyond the gear that they're playing. It's a great thing to know that any need you may have, big or small, will be taken care of and addressed quickly.

For touring artists, producers, engineers, and session players interested in becoming a part of the New Vintage Amplifiers family of artists, we are glad to extend our very competitive artist pricing structure and world-class support. To request more information regarding our artist program, please email an electronic press kit and information about yourself, your band, or your studio, such as record sales, past and upcoming tours, YouTube hits, albums you've played on, artists you've worked with, and anything else you may feel we need to know to info@newvintageamps.com.

Please note that we do not offer free product or paid sponsorship deals to any of our artists.

item.title: Apex Series
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Three distinct modern and vintage tones, at the Apex of hand-wired design

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] Modern high gain AND multi-channel operation is something of a rarity in the hand-wired amplifier market. Few manufacturers actually hand-wire anything with this many options or this much gain. This style of amplifiers take a rare amount of discipline and patience to hand-build with the precision and noise performance New Vintage guitar amps are known for. [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] Introducing the Apex Series. The Apex Series is our 3 channel take on amplifier design that is at the top in tonal options. Developed with producer Josh Barber (Norma Jean, The Devil Wears Prada, War Of Ages), together we fine-tuned each channel for the utmost in versatility. The Apex offers a fully-featured American-voiced clean channel that is dynamic and full with the right amount of top-end sparkle to cut through the mix (think Blackface with a touch of Plexi bite). It's rare to find a clean channel this rich and punchy on a multi-channel amplifier. Channel 2 is ideal for an overdriven rhythm tone with a thick British crunch that is tight and impactful when palm muted (pushed/overdriven Plexi tones). Channel 3 is the modern high gain show-stopper that is smooth, articulate, and percussive (SLO, 5150, early Dual Rec). The completely saturated tone has incredible note definition, sustain and clarity. [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] Unique to the Apex is ALL THREE channels have fully independent signal paths, with no shared tube stages (shared stages are a common trait with nearly all multi-channel amplifiers). This allowed us to give each channel it's own voice, without compromise. Global Resonance and Bright controls shape the overall feel and weight, allowing you to dial in a tone perfect for your style of play. Pair it up with our exclusive 3 button MIDI footswitch for the ultimate in switching options and automation. No matter what sound you're going for, the Apex Series of amplifiers has your bases covered. [/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"] [/bd_col_6] [bd_separator] [bd_col_6 class="alpha"]

Apex 100 Pricing

[/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"]

Apex 50 Pricing

[/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="alpha"]

Headshell - $3,395

Buffered FX Loop (optional) - +$95

MIDI Footswitch (optional) - +$229

[/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"]

Headshell - $3,150

Buffered FX Loop (optional) - +$95

MIDI Footswitch (optional) - +$229

[/bd_col_6] [bd_separator]

Technical Specifications

 
  • 3 Channel All-Tube Amplifier
  • Three Foot-Switchable Channels-Clean, Crunch, Lead
  • 100 Watts of Power (50 Watts With Apex 50)
  • Tube Configuration for Apex 100: 2-6550A, 6-12AX7
  • Tube Configuration for Apex 50: 2-6L6GC, 6-12AX7
  • Channel 1 Controls Include: Volume, Gain, Treble, Mid, Bass
  • Channel 2 Controls Include: Volume, Gain, Treble, Mid, Bass
  • Channel 3 Controls Include: Volume, Gain, Treble, Mid, Bass
  • Body and Brilliance Power Amp EQ Controls Shared Between All 3 Channels
  • 2 Button Analog Footswitch included (All 3 channels accessible with two buttons)
  • Optional Buffered Effects Loop Available
  • Optional 3 Button MIDI Footswitch Available
  • Impedance Switch for 4, 8, and 16 Ohm Cabinets With 2 Parallel Outs
  • Apex 50 Dimensions: 23.375W x 10D x 9H, 43 Lbs
  • Apex 100 Dimensions: 23.375W x 10D x 9H, 45 Lbs
[bd_separator] [bd_center] Interested in purchasing this amplifier? Email us at info@newvintageamps.com to place your order. [/bd_center]

item.title: Zenith Series
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Reaching the top of both clean and modern high gain tones

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] A hand-wired multi-channel high gain amp? The Zenith series of amplifiers offers all of that and more. Back in 2008, we introduced our most popular amp ever, the H&B 50. The Zenith is a culmination of everything we've taken from the evolution of that design and built upon it. Featuring a powerful clean channel and a burning gain channel, this amp is the definition of tonal versatility. We've always felt that many high-gain amps feature a clean channel that is more of an afterthought than a useable function, so we designed the Zenith to excel in both areas. Reminiscent of classic mid-60's American designs, the clean channel showcases both a great top-end sparkle and a strong bottom-end. Stomp on the footswitch and you've got anything from a vintage British crunch to modern high-gain available in the second channel. The Zenith has great touch sensitivity, allowing you to clean up your tone or really bite down all with the way that you play your guitar. Add in the optional buffered effects loop and you've got a guitar amp that is a match for even the most demanding players. Also available in a 25 Watt model. [/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"] [/bd_col_6]

Tone Samples

  [bd_separator] [bd_col_6 class="alpha"]

Zenith 50 Pricing

[/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"]

Zenith 25 Pricing

[/bd_col_6] [bd_col_3 class="alpha"]

Headshell - $2,925 1x12 Combo - $3,250

[/bd_col_3] [bd_col_3]

2x12 Combo - $3,450 Buffered FX Loop - +$95

[/bd_col_3] [bd_col_3]

Headshell - $2,795 1x12 Combo - $3,050

[/bd_col_3] [bd_col_3 class="omega"]

2x12 Combo - $3,250 Buffered FX Loop - +$95

[/bd_col_3] [bd_center] Please Email us at info@newvintageamps.com for pricing and availability. [/bd_center] [bd_separator]

technical specifications for the Zenith 50 and Zenith 25

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"]

Zenith 50

  • 2 Channel Foot-Switchable High Gain Amplifier
  • 50 Watts of All Tube Power
  • Channel 1 is Clean while Channel 2 is Modern High Gain
  • Tube Configuration: 2-EL34 or 2-6L6, 4-12AX7
  • Impedance Switch for 4, 8, and 16 Ohm Cabinets With 2 Parallel Outs
  • Controls Include: Channel 1 (clean): Volume, Gain, Treble, Mid, Bass, & Brightness
  • Channel 2 (gain): Gain, Volume, Treble, Mid, & Bass - (shares Brightness with Ch. 1)
  • Hand-Wired, Using Point to Point and Eyelet Construction
  • Available With Optional Buffered Effects Loop (+$95 additional)
[/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"]

Zenith 25

  • 2 Channel Foot-Switchable High Gain Amplifier
  • 25 Watts of All Tube Power
  • Channel 1 is Clean while Channel 2 is Modern High Gain
  • Tube Configuration: 2-6V6, 4-12AX7
  • Impedance Switch for 4, 8, and 16 Ohm Cabinets With 2 Parallel Outs
  • Controls Include: Channel 1 (clean): Gain, Volume, Treble, Mid, Bass, & Brightness
  • Channel 2 (gain): Gain, Volume, Treble, Mid, & Bass - (shares Brightness with Ch. 1)
  • Hand-Wired, Using Point to Point and Eyelet Construction
  • Available With Optional Buffered Effects Loop (+$95 additional)
[/bd_col_6] [bd_separator] [bd_center] Interested in purchasing this piece? Email us at info@newvintageamps.com to place your order. [/bd_center]

item.title: New Cabinet Giveaway
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Who likes free stuff?

We are teaming up with Gear Talk and Gear Guts to give away a custom 2x12 guitar cabinet! This giveaway is pretty simple - design YOUR custom cabinet in the Customizer below, take a screen shot of the cabinet, and share it on your social media pages (Twitter, Instagram, Facebook) with the hashtag #CustomCabGiveaway and tag the three of us in the post. That's it. There are no limits on the number of entries you can submit as long as you abide by the previously mentioned rules. One lucky person will win the cabinet that they designed and we'll give it to them. How rad is that? Very.

You can find us on Facebook, Twitter, and Instagram by searching "New Vintage Amps."

[bd_col_6 class="alpha"]

RULES

  1. Follow New Vintage Amps, Gear Talk, and Gear Guts
  2. Design your cabinet using the Customizer and options below.
  3. Take a screen shot of the cabinet
  4. Share it on your social media pages (Twitter, Instagram, Facebook) with the hashtag #CustomCabGiveaway
  5. Tag New Vintage Amps, Gear Talk, and Gear Guts
[/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"]

FINE PRINT

The winner will be randomly selected on February 22, 2017. We will notify the winner via the social media outlet upon which their winning submission was posted, so PLEASE follow us on Twitter and Instagram (@newvintageamps) and "like" us on Facebook. We will cover shipping to the lower 48 States. All other shipping will be sorted out between us and the winner. The winner will be contacted about speaker selection for the cabinet. [/bd_col_6]

item.title: CK30
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Blending American & British tones

  [bd_col_6 class="alpha"] The CK30 evolved over the course of several years thanks to Dustin Kensrue (Thrice), Matt Hoopes (Relient K), Cody Jones (LA session player), and many others. Previously only a "secret menu" item, due to popular demand, we've added the CK30 to our core lineup. Starting out life as our MK36 model, various mods and customizations over the course of five years gave this design a life of it's own ("CK" had a bit better ring to it than "Custom MK", right?). Imagine being able to blend chime-y AC style tones with the clarity and depth of the early 60's Blackface amplifiers (B-man). Couple that with a cathode-biased pair of EL34's to provide some incredible power amp dynamics and crunch (a little JTM flavor, anyone?), and you have the CK30. Immediately from the input jack, your signal is split to two very distinct gain stage paths. "Bright" has traditional AC circuit values, while the "Normal" channel has the American voice. Each channel can be run independently, or blended, for an incredibly wide tonal palette of bright and complex cleans, or over the top gain. This combination of two distinctly voiced preamps, coupled with the tone and feel of the power amp, this is an amp that needs to be played, and heard, to believe. [/bd_col_6] [bd_col_6 class="omega"] [caption id="attachment_2044" align="alignnone" width="400"] CK30 (shown with optional 2X12 cabinet)[/caption] [/bd_col_6] [bd_separator]

Tone Samples

  [bd_separator]

CK30 Pricing

 

Headshell - $2,495 1x12 Combo - $2,825 2x12 Combo - $2,995 Buffered effects loop - +$95

[bd_center] Please Email us at info@newvintageamps.com for pricing and availability. [/bd_center] [bd_separator]

Technical Specifications

 
  • Single Channel Tube Amplifier
  • 30 Watts of Power
  • Tube Configuration: 2-EL34, 3-12AX7
  • Controls Include: Volume, Bright gain, Normal gain, Treble, Midrange, Bass and Cut
  • The Bright (AC-voiced) and Normal (American 60's-voiced) "channels" are connected internally and can be blended together or used separately, allowing for 3 different voices from the preamp
  • Both channels have full access to EQ controls
  • Impedance Switch for 4, 8, and 16 Ohm Cabinets With 2 Parallel Outs
  • Dimensions (1x12 combo): 25W x 10D x 20-1/4H
  • Dimensions (Headshell): 25W x 10D x 9H
[bd_separator] [bd_center] Interested in purchasing this piece? Email us at info@newvintageamps.com to place your order. [/bd_center]
